title: Minimize the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with the potentially dangerous NET_RAW capability.
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. By default this can include potentially dangerous capabilities. With Docker as the container runtime the NET_RAW capability is enabled which may be misused by malicious containers. Ideally, all containers should drop this capability. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers with the NET_RAW capability. If you need to run containers with this capability, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods with containers which run with the NET_RAW capability will not be permitted.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that at least one policy disallows the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability. This audit may also require that you review the securityContext in the Pod specification yaml.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of containers with the NET_RAW capability. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the creation of containers with the NET_RAW capability. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/ 2. https://www.nccgroup.trust/uk/our-research/abusing-privileged-and-unprivileged- linux-containers/
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which run with the NET_RAW capability will not be permitted.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_net_raw_capability_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **Kubernetes API calls / kubectl SDK methods:**
  - Use `kubectl get psp` (if PodSecurityPolicy is enabled) or query Pod Security Admission (PSA) labels on namespaces (`kubectl get ns --show-labels`).
  - Enumerate all PodSecurityPolicies (if present) and/or Pod Security Admission configurations.
  - Enumerate all Kyverno, OPA Gatekeeper, or similar admission controller policies that restrict Linux capabilities.
  - For each policy, check for rules that deny or drop the `NET_RAW` capability in containers.

- **Kubernetes CLI commands:**
  - `kubectl get psp -o yaml` (if PSP is enabled)
  - `kubectl get ns --show-labels`
  - `kubectl get clusterpolicy,policy -A -o yaml` (for Kyverno)
  - `kubectl get constrainttemplates,constraints -A -o yaml` (for Gatekeeper/OPA)

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - PodSecurityPolicies (if enabled)
  - Namespace labels for Pod Security Admission (e.g., `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted`)
  - Admission controller policies (Kyverno, Gatekeeper, etc.)

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - For PSP: `.spec.requiredDropCapabilities` should include `NET_RAW` or `.spec.allowedCapabilities` should NOT include `NET_RAW`.
  - For PSA: Namespaces should have `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted` (or equivalent), which by default drops `NET_RAW`.
  - For Kyverno/Gatekeeper: Policies/constraints must deny pods that do not drop `NET_RAW` or that explicitly add it.
  - For all: Ensure at least one policy is in place cluster-wide or per-namespace that enforces the restriction.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** There exists at least one enforced admission policy (PSP, PSA, Kyverno, Gatekeeper, etc.) in each namespace with user workloads that denies admission of pods/containers with the `NET_RAW` capability, unless explicitly allowed for tightly controlled cases.
  - **Fail:** No such policy exists, or namespaces with user workloads allow pods to run with `NET_RAW` by default.

- **Example:**
  - For Pod Security Admission: All user namespaces have `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted`.
  - For Kyverno: There is a policy with a rule matching containers with `capabilities.add` including `NET_RAW` and denying them.
  - For PSP: All applicable PSPs have `NET_RAW` in `requiredDropCapabilities` or do not allow it in `allowedCapabilities`.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
